sp500.notes: Market near all-time highs, grinding higher amid mixed earnings

dow.notes: Dow Jones at record high territory

market_summary.volume: Below average - participation waning despite new highs

vix.signal: Neutral - volatility low but ticking up from extreme lows

cnn_fear_greed.analysis: Greed territory but not extreme. Market has room to run but approaching levels where caution is warranted.

aaii_sentiment.analysis: Retail sentiment improved from extreme bearish readings. Bullish now above historical average (37.5%) for first time in months.

naaim_exposure.analysis: NAAIM at 96.67% - active managers fully invested. Extremely high reading often acts as contrarian warning signal. Little dry powder left for further buying.

cboe_pcr.analysis: Equity PCR below 1.0 indicates call buying dominance. Index PCR elevated at 1.25 suggests institutional hedging activity. Mixed signal.

put_call_ratio.analysis: Overall PCR near long-term average. No extreme fear or greed in options market.

nyse_advance_decline.analysis: NYSE A/D line hit new high Wednesday, confirming market breadth. However, note that progress has been narrow since mid-April.

margin_debt.analysis: Margin debt declining for 3 months. Deleveraging trend continues - historically positive for reducing systemic risk.

leverage_ratio.analysis: Leverage ratio declining from extremes but still above historical average. De-risking ongoing but not complete.

high_yield_spread.analysis: HY spreads at 415 bps, down from 425. Credit markets not flashing warning signals. Risk appetite intact.

mutual_fund_cash.analysis: Cash levels at 3.2% - below historical average. Low cash = less buying power but also reflects confidence.

investment_advisor.analysis: Investment advisors moderately bullish. Not at extreme levels.

ici_fund_flows.analysis: Money market inflows slowing but still positive. Some rotation from international to US equities.

mm_bull_bear.analysis: SentimenTrader MM Bull/Bear indicator remains in bullish territory.

retail_sentiment.analysis: AAII improved from extreme bearishness. Bullish above historical average for first time in months. No longer a strong contrarian buy signal.

institutional_sentiment.analysis: NAAIM at 96.67% - extreme exposure. Active managers all-in. This is a caution flag - when they are all in, who is left to buy?

options_sentiment.analysis: PCR near average. Options market not showing extremes.

fear_greed_assessment.analysis: Fear & Greed at 66 - greed territory but not extreme (76+). Market can continue higher but upside may be limited.

key_divergences: NAAIM extremely high (96.67%) while AAII only moderately bullish (38.3%) - professional vs retail disconnect
NYSE A/D line at new highs but volume declining - breadth/volume divergence
Market at record highs but VIX ticking up - potential early warning

trump_policy.current: Trade negotiations ongoing with China, India, Japan. Market appears to have priced in tariff resolution optimism.
trump_policy.market_impact: Positive - markets rallying on trade deal hopes
trump_policy.sentiment_impact: Improved from tariff panic in early 2025

fed_policy.current: Fed holding rates. Market pricing in potential rate cuts later in 2026 if economic data weakens.
fed_policy.market_impact: Neutral to positive - stable rates support valuations
fed_policy.sentiment_impact: No panic about Fed policy currently

geopolitical.current: Iran war continues but market has largely moved past initial shock. Oil prices elevated but stable.
geopolitical.market_impact: Contained - market resilient despite ongoing conflict

trade_war.current: US-China talks continue. Some progress reported but no comprehensive deal yet.

dashboard.risk_level: Moderate - NAAIM extreme reading is primary concern
dashboard.recommendation: Neutral weight. Market can continue higher but NAAIM extreme + greed territory suggest limiting new longs. Contrarian opportunity has passed - AAII no longer at extreme bearishness.

key_events: CPI data release later this week
sentiment_watch: Monitor if CNN F&G pushes into extreme greed (76+)
Track AAII for reversal back below 37.5% bullish
NAAIM >95% for 2+ weeks is unsustainable - watch for first pullback
